title: Export Metrics Glossary as DBT/PowerBI Compatible YAML
story: As a data engineer, I want to export my metrics glossary in YAML format compatible with DBT and PowerBI, so that I can integrate metrics definitions into my data pipeline tooling.
description:
Add a YAML export option for the metrics glossary that produces output compatible with DBT's `metrics.yml` format and PowerBI semantic models.

### YAML Structure (DBT v2 format)
```yaml
version: 2
metrics:
  - name: snake_case_metric_name
    label: Human Readable Name
    description: Metric definition
    model: ref('generic_model')
    calculation_method: expression
    expression: logic formula
    timestamp: date_field_name or created_at
    time_grains: [day, week, month]
    dimensions: []
    tags: [topic, line_of_business]
```

### Field Mapping
- **name**: metric name converted to snake_case
- **label**: original metric name
- **description**: metric definition
- **expression**: logic field
- **timestamp**: date field name or `created_at` default
- **tags**: derived from Topic and Line of Business fields

A reference PHP script using `Symfony\Component\Yaml\Yaml` is provided in the ticket for guidance.
acceptance_criteria: Metrics can be exported as a .yml file
YAML follows DBT v2 metrics format
Metric name is converted to snake_case
Tags are derived from Topic and Line of Business fields
Generated YAML is valid and parseable
ux_changes: New YAML export option added to the metrics glossary page download/export menu.
middleware_changes: Backend generates YAML output by transforming metric records into the DBT v2 metrics schema using Symfony YAML component.
implementation_notes: Developer had questions about mapping: label (uses Name), model (generic placeholder), calculation_method (expression), timestamp (created_at default), expression and time_grains. These were clarified and implementation was submitted for review on development.
comment_summary: Developer implemented the YAML export and had clarifying questions about field mappings (label, model, timestamp, expression, time_grains). After clarification, the export was completed and submitted for review on development.
